topic: In China employee termination: troubleshooting checklist for payroll release control

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Need for lawful basis and evidence integrity

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Termination-month closure and social insurance processing obligations

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Payroll tax treatment context for final-pay components

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Operational process and documentation controls for multinational employers

consensus: Termination payroll quality is primarily an execution-control issue.
Checklist and troubleshooting governance reduces avoidable disputes and rework.
Contested components should be isolated and escalated, not rushed into release.
uncertainties: City-level evidence thresholds and dispute handling can vary by facts.
Some treatment details require local confirmation before release.
English-language summaries may not reflect all local implementation nuance.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Use a checklist-first release workflow plus troubleshooting matrix to standardize decisions under payroll pressure.
